free_text: Study of the (d,p) reaction in the 1P Shell
E(dE/dX) telescopes consisting of silicon
surface-barrier detectors. The (delta E) counters
approx. 150 um thick, while (E) counters were over
2000 um thick.
Broad-range spectrograph with photographic
emulsions.
Uncertainty includes statistical and
systematic errors
Tables I, VI
Compilation produced by RFNC-VNIIEF Sarov
Correction Monitor Line in A1450002.
Last checking has been done.
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
ERR-T -> DATA-ERR.
Target was made by evaporating enriched 6Li onto thin
(above 20 ug/cm2) carbon substrates in a bell jar.
In order to reduce oxygen contamination, the Li
targets were transferred through a vacuum lock and
mounted in the scattering chamber under vacuum.
In addition targets of LiF and CaF2 were used in
determination of absolute cross sections.
The yield of elastic deuterons was compared with
that from pure elemental targets and with that
obtained from a CaF2 target. The measured cross
section of deuterons elastically scattered from
Ca40 was then used together with the known chemical
composition of the targets to obtain absolute cross
sections (see REL-REF).
See ANALYSIS
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated.
Target was made by evaporating enriched 6Li onto thin
(above 20 ug/cm2) carbon substrates in a bell jar.
In order to reduce oxygen contamination, the Li
targets were transferred through a vacuum lock and
mounted in the scattering chamber under vacuum.
In addition targets of LiF and CaF2 were used in
determination of absolute cross sections.
The yield of elastic deuterons was compared with
that from pure elemental targets and with that
obtained from a CaF2 target. The measured cross
section of deuterons elastically scattered from
Ca40 was then used together with the known chemical
composition of the targets to obtain absolute cross
sections (see REL-REF).
See ANALYSIS
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated.
The 11B targets were self-supporting, prepared from
enriched 11B.
The (d,p) cross section at extreme forward angles was
measured at 12.0 MeV relative to the 11.8 MeV
Heidelberg measurement at 55 degr., because at extreme
forward angles there were large uncertainties in the
results at 11.8 MeV.
Only the
peak cross sections and cross section at extreme
forward angles have been re-measured because there
were large uncertainties in the Schmidt-Rohr's
results at 11.8 MeV.
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated.
The 11B targets were self-supporting, prepared from
enriched 11B.
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated.
The C targets consisted of self-supported carbon films
of natural abundance.
In addition targets of CF2 and CaF2 were used in
determination of absolute cross sections.
The yield of elastic deuterons was compared with
that from pure elemental targets and with that
obtained from a CaF2 target. The measured cross
section of deuterons elastically scattered from
Ca40 was then used together with the known chemical
composition of the targets to obtain absolute cross
sections (see REL-REF).
See ANALYSIS
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated.
The C targets consisted of self-supported carbon films,
either natural abundance or enriched to appr. 52% 13C.
For C13, the cross section
was obtained relative to C12 with a natural
carbon target.
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated.
14N targets consisted of melamine evaporated onto a
carbon substrate. Typical target thickness was approx.
300 ug/cm2.
